entries.definition:
sea); to be free of impurity, to be free of guilt, to be pious, to be devoted; to fulfil one’s promise, wheat, goodness. Of this root, six forms occur 32 times in the Qur’an: تبرّ tabarr twice; بر barr (1) three times; أبرار ʾabrār six times; بِرّ birr eight times; بَرَرَة bararatun once and بر barr (2) 12 times.
تبرّ tabarr [imperf. of v. برّ barra] I [intrans.] to be charitable, to act charitably, to do good (2:224) ولا تجعلوا الله عرضة لأيمانكم أن تبروا [believers] do not allow your God-backed oaths to hinder you from performing a charitable act/from doing good II [trans.] to show kindness to (60:8) وأن تبروهم وتقسطوا إليهم and that you should show them kindness and deal justly with them.

بر barr (1) I [act. part.] 1 one who carries out acts of kindness, one who is devoted, one acting dutifully towards kinsfolk (19:32) وبراً بوالدتي and [made me] cherish my mother 2 one who is true to his promise (in one interpretation of 52:28) إنا كنا من قبل ندعوه إنه هو البر الرحيم we used to invoke Him; He is the one true to His promise II [n.; pl. أبرار ʾabrār] a pious person, righteous (76:5) إن الأبرار يشربون من كأس كان مزاجها كافوراً the pious shall drink of cups whose mixture is camphor; *(52:28) البر [attribute of God] the Good, the Benign; the one true to His Promise.

بر birr I [n./v. n.] righteousness, piety, righteous act, act of charity (2:44) أتأمرون الناس بالبر وتنسون أنفسكم do you bid people towards piety and forget [to do it] yourselves? II [n.] the state of being righteous (3:92) لن تنالوا البر حتى تنفقوا مما تحبون you will not/never attain righteousness until you give of what you truly cherish.

بررة bararatun [pl. of act. part. بارّ barr] pious, dutiful, sincere, virtuous (80:16) كرام بررة Noble and virtuous [scribes].

بر barr (2) [n.] land (as opposed to sea-shore) *(6:59) ويعلم ما في البر والبحر He knows all [lit. what is in land and sea].

Of this root, six forms occur 32 times in the Qur’an: تبرّ tabarr twice; بر barr (1) three times; أبرار ʾabrār six times; بِرّ birr eight times; بَرَرَة bararatun once and بر barr (2) 12 times.
تبرّ tabarr [imperf. of v. برّ barra] I [intrans.] to be charitable, to act charitably, to do good (2:224) ولا تجعلوا الله عرضة لأيمانكم أن تبروا [believers] do not allow your God-backed oaths to hinder you from performing a charitable act/from doing good II [trans.] to show kindness to (60:8) وأن تبروهم وتقسطوا إليهم and that you should show them kindness and deal justly with them.

بر barr (1) I [act. part.] 1 one who carries out acts of kindness, one who is devoted, one acting dutifully towards kinsfolk (19:32) وبراً بوالدتي and [made me] cherish my mother 2 one who is true to his promise (in one interpretation of 52:28) إنا كنا من قبل ندعوه إنه هو البر الرحيم we used to invoke Him; He is the one true to His promise II [n.; pl. أبرار ʾabrār] a pious person, righteous (76:5) إن الأبرار يشربون من كأس كان مزاجها كافوراً the pious shall drink of cups whose mixture is camphor; *(52:28) البر [attribute of God] the Good, the Benign; the one true to His Promise.

بر birr I [n./v. n.] righteousness, piety, righteous act, act of charity (2:44) أتأمرون الناس بالبر وتنسون أنفسكم do you bid people towards piety and forget [to do it] yourselves? II [n.] the state of being righteous (3:92) لن تنالوا البر حتى تنفقوا مما تحبون you will not/never attain righteousness until you give of what you truly cherish.

بررة bararatun [pl. of act. part. بارّ barr] pious, dutiful, sincere, virtuous (80:16) كرام بررة Noble and virtuous [scribes].

بر barr (2) [n.] land (as opposed to sea-shore) *(6:59) ويعلم ما في البر والبحر He knows all [lit. what is in land and sea].
